proposed_from.topic: Honest adaptive inference for the optimal treatment-policy value across near-tie and exceptional-law boundaries: derive local experiments over unknown margin exponent/constant, tie-atom mass, and nuisance-smoothness strata; construct one cross-fitted data-adaptive interval uniformly honest over the declared union; characterize and attain the minimax expected-length adaptation diagram, with matching causal lower bounds and Liu-Zhang-Nie-Soon's rilpivirine-versus-efavirenz individualized-regime analysis as consumer
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"No active proposal studies a randomized-sign sparse near-tie mixture whose observed causal laws are explicitly verified to generate a strict expected-length adaptation penalty at an exact-tie optimal-value law.","published_axis":"The closest optimal-value papers establish procedures or fixed-condition guarantees, while Cai--Low establish generic interval lower-bound machinery. The proposed advance is conditional on proving the displayed causal mixture theorem: the full-data membership audit, product chi-square calculation, and strict-penalty regime \\(3s_1\\alpha<d\\), not a claim that this threshold is globally sharp or a claim of priority for smoothing, root-\\(n\\) inference, or generic ordered-modulus theory."}

seed_details.seed: Define an ordered observed-data causal modulus for optimal-value confidence intervals over a finite union of margin, tie-mass, and Hölder-smoothness strata; prove matching lower and upper expected-length bounds.
seed_details.anchor_read: Xu and Guo motivate nonregular optimal-value inference and leave unrestricted optimality beyond their robust asymptotically linear unbiased class unresolved; Luedtke and van der Laan isolate exceptional laws and point to inference beyond fixed-law regularity.
seed_details.red_team: Immediate from Cai and Low because ordered moduli already characterize adaptive intervals.
seed_details.rebuttal: Cai--Low treats linear functionals in Gaussian models; the causal value is a max of estimated regressions, has a tie atom, and requires a new testing reduction plus cross-fitted bias control.

seed_details.seed: Derive the local experiment for sequences with local blip drift and changing tie-atom mass, then characterize local minimax confidence length.
seed_details.anchor_read: Hirano and Porter show why local unbiasedness fails at nondifferentiable targets and motivate local asymptotic minimax criteria; Luedtke and van der Laan characterize the fixed-law exceptional boundary.
seed_details.red_team: It is only the standard Gaussian shift experiment after conditioning on covariates.
seed_details.rebuttal: A positive or varying tie atom makes the limit depend on the max kink and outcome residual variation; whether the limit is Gaussian is itself substantive.

seed_details.seed: Construct a cross-fitted interval uniformly honest over an unknown margin and nuisance-smoothness union using smoothing-bias critical values.
seed_details.anchor_read: Xu and Guo give adaptive smoothing under a scale-gap condition; Armstrong and Kolesár show that honesty requires explicit worst-case bias accounting.
seed_details.red_team: A Lepski selection plus a bias bound is routine.
seed_details.rebuttal: The unknown tie atom makes the selected smoothing bias and stochastic term interact through a nonregular causal functional.

seed_details.seed: Characterize the local law and interval length for finite-action optimal values under arbitrary partial-tie patterns.
seed_details.anchor_read: Whitehouse et al. formulate the problem as a maximum of nuisance scores; Xu and Guo restrict their main efficiency analysis to binary treatment.
seed_details.red_team: Softmax already handles any finite maximum.
seed_details.rebuttal: Computing a smooth maximum does not characterize the active-face-specific local experiment or honest length at changing partial ties.

seed_details.seed: Give uniformly honest optimized-regime intervals under monotone coarsening and near ties in the rilpivirine-versus-efavirenz analysis.
seed_details.anchor_read: Liu et al. use augmented IPW and cross-validation to address coarsening and resubstitution bias; Luedtke and van der Laan handle the separate exceptional-law obstacle.
seed_details.red_team: This is merely an influence-function augmentation of existing work.
seed_details.rebuttal: The interaction is not published, but the clinical coarsening layer obscures the cleaner general inference contribution.

seed_details.seed: Characterize a semiparametric efficiency frontier beyond robust asymptotically linear unbiased estimators at exceptional laws.
seed_details.anchor_read: Xu and Guo explicitly restrict their variance lower bound to a robust asymptotically linear unbiased class; Hirano and Porter explain why conventional regular-efficiency criteria fail at kinks.
seed_details.red_team: Hirano--Porter already rules out the desired efficiency theory.
seed_details.rebuttal: A different local decision criterion may exist, but it requires first solving the local experiment.

seed_details.seed: Derive a joint policy-regret and confidence-length frontier for subagging versus smoothing optimal-regime learning.
seed_details.anchor_read: Shi, Lu, and Song establish nominal coverage and selected MSE and length comparisons for subagging.
seed_details.red_team: This changes the target from fixed optimal-value inference to policy learning.
seed_details.rebuttal: No direct rebuttal preserves the requested estimand.

seed_details.seed: Invert a treatment-label permutation or conformal test for the optimal value at exceptional laws.
seed_details.anchor_read: The anchor papers identify nonregularity under observational or semiparametric nuisance estimation rather than a finite randomization group.
seed_details.red_team: Exact symmetry is absent once an optimal rule is estimated from covariates and observational propensity scores.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only a restrictive randomized-trial submodel restores the symmetry, which is not the stated general problem.

proposed_from.literature_map: The chosen angle is not subsumed by Luedtke and van der Laan (2016), which gives fixed-law pathwise-differentiability and root-n inference; Shi, Lu, and Song (2020), which gives nominal subagging coverage; Xu and Guo (2026), which gives estimator-class variance optimality; or Whitehouse et al. (2025), which gives softmax inference. Cai and Low (2004) supplies the ordered-modulus adaptation method to adapt, while Armstrong and Kolesár (2020) supplies bias-aware honesty logic. The closest unresolved gap is the causal ordered modulus and its attainable interval over a union containing jointly varying margin, tie-mass, and smoothness strata.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:sparse-near-tie-adaptation-lower-bound / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The strict lower-bound claim is confined to the explicit two-stratum construction and 3s_1α<d; it does not claim a global adaptation diagram or attainment.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Strict slower-than-root-n expected-length penalty at P_0^∘ conditional on the displayed sparse family, fixed class parameters, and 3s_1α<d.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:matching-union-interval
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: No matching connected union-honest upper interval is asserted; it remains an explicitly labeled open-ended construction problem.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The lower theorem is not represented as a sharp attained frontier unless this OEQ is resolved.

banked_reason: User-directed D0 pipeline test completed normally after three solve rounds; the sound subfield result was intentionally stopped with five gated proposals and two open obligations still pending.
banked_proposal_promise_gap: A matched union-honest upper interval and the exact expected-length frontier remain open; D0 itself dispatched, rendered, gated narrowing proposals, preserved provisional proofs, and persisted obligations correctly.
